speaker: HOST
text: Master Fenton, talk not to me. My mind is

speaker: HOST
text: heavy. I will give over all.

speaker: FENTON
text: Yet hear me speak. Assist me in my purpose,

speaker: FENTON
text: And, as I am a gentleman, I’ll give thee

speaker: FENTON
text: A hundred pound in gold more than your loss.

speaker: HOST
text: I will hear you, Master Fenton, and I will, at the

speaker: HOST
text: least, keep your counsel.

speaker: FENTON
text: From time to time I have acquainted you

speaker: FENTON
text: With the dear love I bear to fair Anne Page,

speaker: FENTON
text: Who mutually hath answered my affection,

speaker: FENTON
text: So far forth as herself might be her chooser,

speaker: FENTON
text: Even to my wish. I have a letter from her

speaker: FENTON
text: Of such contents as you will wonder at,

speaker: FENTON
text: The mirth whereof so larded with my matter

speaker: FENTON
text: That neither singly can be manifested

speaker: FENTON
text: Without the show of both. Fat Falstaff

speaker: FENTON
text: Hath a great scene; the image of the jest

speaker: FENTON
text: I’ll show you here at large.

speaker: FENTON
text: Hark, good mine Host:

speaker: FENTON
text: Tonight at Herne’s oak, just ’twixt twelve and one,

speaker: FENTON
text: Must my sweet Nan present the Fairy Queen—

speaker: FENTON
text: The purpose why is here—in which disguise,

speaker: FENTON
text: While other jests are something rank on foot,

speaker: FENTON
text: Her father hath commanded her to slip

speaker: FENTON
text: Away with Slender, and with him at Eton

speaker: FENTON
text: Immediately to marry. She hath consented. Now, sir,

speaker: FENTON
text: Her mother, ever strong against that match

speaker: FENTON
text: And firm for Doctor Caius, hath appointed

speaker: FENTON
text: That he shall likewise shuffle her away,

speaker: FENTON
text: While other sports are tasking of their minds,

speaker: FENTON
text: And at the dean’ry, where a priest attends,

speaker: FENTON
text: Straight marry her. To this her mother’s plot

speaker: FENTON
text: She, seemingly obedient, likewise hath

speaker: FENTON
text: Made promise to the doctor. Now, thus it rests:

speaker: FENTON
text: Her father means she shall be all in white,

speaker: FENTON
text: And in that habit, when Slender sees his time

speaker: FENTON
text: To take her by the hand and bid her go,

speaker: FENTON
text: She shall go with him. Her mother hath intended

speaker: FENTON
text: The better to denote her to the doctor—

speaker: FENTON
text: For they must all be masked and vizarded—

speaker: FENTON
text: That quaint in green she shall be loose enrobed,

speaker: FENTON
text: With ribbons pendent flaring ’bout her head;

speaker: FENTON
text: And when the doctor spies his vantage ripe,

speaker: FENTON
text: To pinch her by the hand, and on that token

speaker: FENTON
text: The maid hath given consent to go with him.

speaker: HOST
text: Which means she to deceive, father or mother?

speaker: FENTON
text: Both, my good Host, to go along with me.

speaker: FENTON
text: And here it rests, that you’ll procure the vicar

speaker: FENTON
text: To stay for me at church ’twixt twelve and one,

speaker: FENTON
text: And, in the lawful name of marrying,

speaker: FENTON
text: To give our hearts united ceremony.

speaker: HOST
text: Well, husband your device. I’ll to the vicar.

speaker: HOST
text: Bring you the maid, you shall not lack a priest.

speaker: FENTON
text: So shall I evermore be bound to thee;

speaker: FENTON
text: Besides, I’ll make a present recompense.
